meaning: n.炫学,假装学者,卖弄学问
examples: The book is a demonstration of scholarship without pedantry.
Nor is the distinction a matter of pedantry.
Pedantry is the unseasonable ostentation of learning.
"If the intent and moral vision do not exist, then the rules become meaningless pedantry, " he argues.
When I responded that children and pensioners are not producers but they are consumers I was accused of pedantry.
